2606.20776.title: Formally Verified Code Synthesis for Structured Data Translation in a Medical Internet of Things
2606.20776.paper_summary: In this work we present a LLM powered, evolutionary code synthesis system for structured data translation in a Medical Internet of Things settings. A key challenge in this domain is ensuring that the synthesized code is trustworthy and reliable. To this end, we integrate a formal verification step into our code synthesis pipeline to ensure that any generated code is guaranteed to satisfy predefined requirements. In particular, we present a case study of integrating a novel device (a pulse oximeter) into the existing network of devices. Our system generates a formally verified translation between the device's JSON schema and the Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR) format used by the wider system. This formal verification stage ensures structured data translated by the generated code will always be in the target output schema. We provide a set of experimental results which demonstrate that our system is able to consistently generate correct translation at low cost.
2606.20776.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們提出了一個基於大型語言模型的進化代碼合成系統，用於醫療物聯網環境中的結構化數據翻譯。這個領域的一個主要挑戰是確保合成的代碼是可信和可靠的。為此，我們在代碼合成流程中整合了一個形式驗證步驟，以確保任何生成的代碼都能滿足預定的要求。特別地，我們展示了一個案例研究，將一個新型設備（脈搏血氧計）整合到現有的設備網絡中。我們的系統生成了一個在設備的JSON架構和更廣泛系統使用的快速醫療互操作性資源（FHIR）格式之間的形式驗證翻譯。這個形式驗證階段確保了由生成的代碼翻譯的結構化數據將始終符合目標輸出架構。我們提供了一組實驗結果，顯示我們的系統能夠以低成本穩定地生成正確的翻譯。
2606.20776.author: Colin Samplawski et.al.
